title: US warns ships paying Iran transit fees in Hormuz face sanctions
summary: Washington warned that commercial vessels paying tolls or fees to Iran for Strait of Hormuz transit could face US sanctions, as the administration launches a new 'Maritime Freedom Construct' coalition to reopen the critical waterway.
contents:
## US Threatens Sanctions on Ships Paying Iran Hormuz Transit Fees

### New Sanctions Warning
The United States warned on May 2 that commercial ships paying tolls or fees to Iran for passage through the Strait of Hormuz could face sanctions. The warning targets a reported Iranian scheme to categorize vessels as "hostile," "neutral," or "friendly" based on their compliance with Iranian demands.

### Maritime Freedom Construct
Washington announced a new international coalition dubbed the "Maritime Freedom Construct" aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic. The initiative runs parallel to a separate European-led effort organized by France and Britain, which French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot described as "complementary" rather than competing.

### Strait Status
The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to unescorted commercial traffic, with approximately one-fifth of the world's traded oil and gas normally transiting the waterway. Oil prices remain more than 50% above pre-war levels as traders face prolonged disruption.

### New Oil Sanctions
The State Department imposed additional measures on entities linked to Iranian petroleum exports, including China-based Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal, accused of importing millions of barrels of sanctioned crude and enabling billions in revenue for Tehran. Beijing rejected the measures as unlawful unilateral sanctions.

**Source:** Al Jazeera, Reuters, National Post
